title: CYLINDER HEAD

figures_detected.description: Photo showing cylinder head area with arrow indicating a component on the engine
Rocker cover bolt removal numerical order diagram showing bolt positions numbered 1-8
Cylinder head bolt removal numerical order diagram showing bolt positions numbered 1-10

content_blocks.text: After removing timing belt, do not turn crankshaft and camshaft separately, or valves will strike piston heads.
When installing camshaft, oil seals or valve lifters, lubricate contacting surfaces with new engine oil.

steps.text: Drain coolant from radiator and cylinder block. Be careful not to spill coolant on drive belt.
Remove timing belt. Refer to "TIMING BELT", (EM-5012).
Disconnect front exhaust pipe from manifold.
Disconnect spill hose, glow plug cable, EGR hose and coolant temperature sensor connectors.
Remove air duct and air cleaner.
Disconnect vacuum hoses, coolant hoses, fuel hoses from thermostat housing and vacuum pump.
Remove intake manifold support and dipstick tube bolt.
Disconnect fast idle device wire from injection pump.
Remove rocker cover bolts in numerical order as shown in the figure.
Remove cylinder head bolts in numerical order as shown in the figure.
warnings: Head warpage or cracking could result from removing in incorrect order.
Cylinder head bolts should be loosened in two or three stages.

steps.text: Remove cylinder head complete with manifolds.

model_notes: VJZ engine variant. Procedure applies to diesel engine with injection pump and glow plugs.
